page_description: Everything speakers need to know to apply for Devcon 8 India — code of conduct, session lengths, programming values, the review process, and how decisions are made.
heading: Devcon 8 India: Speaker application guidelines

important_information.title: Important information
important_information.body:
- This year we'll be accepting fewer talks compared to Devcon 7. That said, there are more ways to participate, including discussion corners and [Community Hubs](https://forum.devcon.org/t/rfp-13-devcon-8-india-community-hubs/8657).
- Submit your proposal only once, no need to duplicate it among all tracks/format. You can add more context in the Notes section of your proposal.
- Each application will be reviewed by a committee of experts. Their decision is final. There is no appeal process.
- The review committee may suggest converting your session to a different format (for example, a Talk to a Lightning Talk or vice versa) if it better fits the program. We will always reach out before making any changes.
- All speakers and panelists listed in your proposal must be confirmed at the time of submission. Do not include tentative or unconfirmed participants.
- All accepted speakers hosting Talks, Mixed Format sessions or Workshops will receive a free ticket to attend Devcon. Lightning Talk speakers will be able to purchase a discounted ticket. If you already purchased a ticket by the time you receive your confirmation, [contact us](mailto:support@devcon.org) to arrange a refund.
- Review the [Talks Wishlists](https://ef-events.notion.site/devcon-8-talks-wishlist) for ideas of topics per track.

code_of_conduct.title: Speakers code of conduct, tldr;
code_of_conduct.body:
Please read the general [code of conduct](https://devcon.org/en/code-of-conduct/) and especially the "Topics to Avoid" aka:

- **Promotional or purely "commercial" talks will not be accepted. All talks must be educational.** Devcon is not a place to shill your solution, it is about sharing knowledge.

session_lengths.title: Session lengths
session_lengths.body:
**Session lengths depend on the type of session:**

- Lightning Talks (5 minutes + 2 minutes for Q&A)
- Talks (20 minutes + 5 minutes for Q&A)
- Mixed Formats (25 minutes)
- Workshops (60 minutes)

**Note:** The organizers may suggest adjustments to your session length based on content and program needs.

programming_values.title: Devcon programming values
programming_values.body:
Devcon takes a holistic approach and aims to engage all attendees.

Those who want to learn will find the type of content best-suited to them whether it be talks, workshops or free-form discussions. Below are some values that guide Devcon's programming:

1. **Collaboration and coordination** — Cross pollination and alignment on ideas and (remote) people
2. **Education and inspiration** — Provide educational & inspirational content, including newcomers
3. **Get inspired by and inspire adjacent communities** — Learn from FOSS best practices, inspire others to follow our standards
4. **Challenge our understanding of Ethereum as it is** — Bring novel perspectives & approaches, update on state-of-the-art research

review_process.title: Review process
review_process.body:
- Each track has its own reviewing committee, consisting of a track lead and a diverse set of reviewers.
- Each application will be reviewed by multiple members of the review team (2 minimum).
- Talks will be selected on aggregate scores from the review criteria.

review_criteria.title: Review criteria
review_criteria.body:
When assessing submissions, Reviewers will keep the **Devcon Programming Values** in mind, and consider the following review criteria:

- **Is the submission information accurate and in the right track?** Is the submission in the right track, with the correct tags?
- **Is the submission focused on sharing knowledge?** All submissions must have the intended purpose of sharing knowledge (experience, learnings, development, research, or a specific problem they are hoping to solve.) **Note:** Reviewers will not accept an application that is devoted to promoting a company or project. If applicants would like to shill a project this is not the place.
- **Is the submission innovative and/or novel?** All talks and submissions should be geared to present new ideas to drive the ecosystem forward, not repeat presentations that have been given several times before.
- **Is the submission constructive?** Does the submission aim to solve a problem or contribute to the ecosystem in some way? Is it providing insight or solutions to a challenge faced by many?
- **Is the speaker qualified?** Does the speaker have sufficient knowledge and experience to present on this topic?
- **Is the speaker relevant to the session?**

decision.title: Decision
decision.body:
**The speaker application process is very competitive** and unfortunately, this may mean that quality talks are not selected due to time and space constraints.

- By the end of August, first decisions will be made. **Expect all decisions to be final by the end of September.**
- You will be notified of the status of your application once all applications have been carefully reviewed by our committee of experts.
- Reviewers' decisions are final, and there is no appeal process, but there are lots of ways to [contribute to Devcon](/) if your application is not accepted.
- All accepted speakers hosting talks, workshops, or mixed format sessions will receive a free ticket to attend Devcon. If you already purchased a ticket by the time you receive your confirmation, [contact us](mailto:support@devcon.org) to arrange a refund.
